---
title: Degree and second degree
jupyter: python3
---

### Set up and load data

```{python}

## Import modules
import abct
import numpy as np
import plotly.express as px
from scipy import io, sparse
from nilearn import plotting
from nctpy import utils, metrics
import bct

## Load data
data = io.loadmat("./data/hcp_data.mat")

```

### Degrees and measures of communication and control

```{python}

W = data["W"]

# Measures
np.random.seed(1)
s = "discrete"
D = {
    "Degree": abct.degree(W, "first"),
    "Second degree": abct.degree(W, "second"),
    "Eigenvector centrality": - sparse.linalg.eigs(W, k=1)[1].real.ravel(),
    "Diffusion efficiency": 1/bct.mean_first_passage_time(W).mean(0),
    "Communicability centrality": np.diag(sparse.linalg.expm(W)),
    "Average control": metrics.ave_control(utils.matrix_normalization(W, s), s),
    "Modal control": metrics.modal_control(W)
    }

```

### Show maps and scatter of first and second degree

```{python}

parc = data["parc"].ravel() - 1
n_hemi = len(parc) // 2

gii_left = "./data/S1200.L.very_inflated_MSMAll.32k_fs_LR.surf.gii"

for i, name in enumerate(["Degree", "Second degree"]):
    Vals = D[name]
    plotting.plot_surf(
        surf_mesh=gii_left,
        surf_map=Vals[parc][:n_hemi],
        view = "lateral",
        title = name,
        cmap = "inferno",
        vmin = np.percentile(Vals[parc][:n_hemi], 5),
        vmax = np.percentile(Vals[parc][:n_hemi], 95),
    )

```

### Compute measures of communication and control

```{python}

Pairs = [["Degree", "Second degree"],
         ["Degree", "Eigenvector centrality"],
         ["Degree", "Diffusion efficiency"],
         ["Second degree", "Communicability centrality"],
         ["Second degree", "Average control"],
         ["Second degree", "Modal control"]]

for i, pair in enumerate(Pairs):
    name1, name2 = pair
    fig = px.scatter(
        x=D[name1],
        y=D[name2],
        labels={"x": name1, "y": name2},
        title=f"r = {np.corrcoef(D[name1], D[name2])[0, 1]:.3f}",
        width=600, height=600,
    )
    fig.update_layout(title_x=0.5).show()

```
